acceptance criteria: visual and final sanity JSON files contain only book-relative paths
all numeric residuals are below tolerance
the shared generic visual-renderer scaffold is absent
chapter goal: Build projective line coordinates and identify the cross-ratio as the invariant of projective reparametrization.
computational checks: harmonic finite and infinity examples equal -1
artifact existence, nonzero size, and nonblank raster statistics
concept inventory: line through two projective points as the span [lambda p + mu q]
RP^1 homogeneous coordinates [x:y] and the finite/infinite chart split
chart transition u=1/t on the overlap
2 x 2 projective transformations and finite Mobius formulas
cross-ratio as a determinant quotient independent of representative scaling
cross-ratio invariance under projective matrices and perspectivities
six cross-ratio values generated by input permutations
harmonic quadruples with cross-ratio -1, including the infinite fourth point
RP^2 determinant formula for collinear point quadruples
implementation notes: All generated files stay under artifacts/chapter-04-lines-and-cross-ratios; no shared helpers, scripts, indexes, source page images, or textbook figures are edited or copied.

library routing table.fallback: plain SVG or matplotlib-only line diagram
library routing table.why: static 2D rays and chart labels need durable inspection

library routing table.fallback: matplotlib pole plot with sampled matrices
library routing table.representation: before/after axis map plus interactive graph
library routing table.why: static artifact records the invariant; Plotly lets learners zoom near denominator poles
the chapter's proof moves are determinant cancellations
the graph exposes the generators x -> 1/x and x -> 1-x

proof-visualization strategy: Use determinant factorization as the algebraic proof scaffold and a six-node permutation graph as the proof-state view for ordered cross-ratio values.
risks: Plotly HTML is larger than static PNG because it is standalone
finite chart labels can hide the projective point at infinity unless the reciprocal chart is shown
source span read: Perspectives on Projective Geometry, Chapter 4, Sections 4.1-4.4, printed pp. 67-78 / PDF pp. 89-100.

visual sequence.concept: projective line coordinates and RP1 charts
visual sequence.learner_inspection_target: Compare the finite chart t with the reciprocal chart u and locate [1:0].
Follow four marked points from input coordinates to image coordinates.
visual sequence.validation_invariant: cross-ratio before and after the matrix action agree

visual sequence.learner_inspection_target: Zoom near a denominator pole and observe the vertical blow-up as an infinity crossing.
visual sequence.validation_invariant: tracked points retain the same cross-ratio for each matrix

visual sequence.learner_inspection_target: Watch the harmonic fourth point d=1/c cross infinity when c=0.
visual sequence.validation_invariant: finite and infinite harmonic examples both have cross-ratio -1

visual sequence.learner_inspection_target: Use the two generator labels to recover all six ordered values.
visual sequence.validation_invariant: symbolic permutation identity reduces to zero

visual sequence.learner_inspection_target: Compare residuals for scaling, Mobius action, RP2 determinants, and projection.
